coreEn.title: Core EN Platform
coreEn.subtitle: One platform replaced every spreadsheet in the office.
coreEn.overview: An engineering design office ran their entire operation on spreadsheets — projects, timesheets, payroll, expenses, everything. We built Core EN to replace all of it. One platform, one source of truth, used by every person in the office, every day.
coreEn.challenge: Every Friday was chaos. Field engineers submitted timesheets on paper. The office manager re-entered them into Excel. Payroll took two full days of manual calculations — tax deductions, insurance, overtime. One mistake meant redoing the entire sheet. Project costs lived in a separate spreadsheet that was always outdated. Client invoices were assembled by hand. The office had outgrown its tools years ago, but every solution they'd tried was either too generic or too complicated to adopt.
coreEn.solution: We didn't build a generic ERP. We sat with the team — engineers, managers, accountants — and mapped exactly how they work. Then we built around their workflows, not the other way around. Engineers log timesheets and request leaves from their devices. Managers approve with one click. Payroll generates automatically with taxes, insurance, and overtime calculated. Invoices pull directly from project data. Every role sees exactly what they need, nothing more.
features: Timesheet logging with overtime tracking — no more paper forms or Friday re-entry
One-click payroll generation — tax, insurance, and deductions calculated automatically
Leave and permission requests with multi-level approval chains
Project cost tracking that stays accurate because it pulls from live data
Invoice generation from project records — PDF export in seconds
Role-based dashboards — engineers, managers, and accountants each see what matters
results: Payroll processing dropped from 2 days to 4 hours
Project setup went from 30 minutes to under 5
First time the office had real-time visibility into margins

wasfa.title: Wasfa
wasfa.subtitle: The app that answers "what's for dinner" before you even ask.
wasfa.overview: Wasfa is a meal planning app for people who want to eat well but hate the mental load of deciding what to cook. Pick recipes, plan your week, get a shopping list — done. Available in English and Arabic.
wasfa.challenge: The weekly cycle: open the fridge, stare at it, close it, order delivery. You know you should plan meals. You've tried — pinned recipes on Pinterest, downloaded apps that wanted you to log macros and count calories. But all you actually need is a simple answer to one question: what am I cooking this week? Nothing on the market respected that simplicity.
wasfa.solution: We stripped meal planning to its core: three actions. Plan Meals. Find Recipes. Build Lists. No calorie tracking, no fitness goals, no onboarding questionnaire. Open the app, browse recipes, drag them into your week, and your shopping list writes itself. The design is intentionally calm — warm greens, clean type, zero clutter. We built it bilingual from day one, with full Arabic and English support, so it feels native in both languages.
features: Drag meals into your weekly plan — the entire week visible at a glance
Browse curated recipes with filters that actually help you decide
Shopping lists generate automatically from your planned meals
Full Arabic and English experience — not a translation, native in both
Opens to your plan, not a settings screen — built for daily use
results: Users save 3+ hours a week they used to spend deciding what to cook
9 out of 10 users finish onboarding on their first launch
4.8-star average rating — users cite simplicity as the #1 reason
Live in 4 countries across English and Arabic markets

items.q: How much does a typical project cost?
items.a: Projects typically range from $15K–$80K depending on scope. We'll give you an honest estimate on the first call — no surprises later.
Most MVPs ship in 8–12 weeks. Complex platforms take 4–6 months. You'll see weekly demos from week one.

items.q: Do you work with early-stage startups?
items.a: Yes — if you have a validated idea and are ready to invest in building it properly. We're not the right fit for 'just exploring' projects.
We don't disappear. We offer ongoing support, monitoring, and iteration packages. Most clients keep us on retainer.
100%. You own everything we build — code, designs, infrastructure. No lock-in, no strings attached.

items.q: What if I'm not happy with the work?
items.a: Weekly demos mean you see progress constantly. If something isn't right, we course-correct immediately — not after months of silent development.
